control_description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software functions, and other protected information assets based on roles and responsibilities, segregation of duties, and least privilege principles.

evidence_files.description: Orion Health Systems data access authorization policy with least-privilege and data classification requirements
PostgreSQL production database role and grant export as of December 20, 2025
CISO-approved exception register with compensating controls for policy deviations
Q4 2025 data access request tickets with approval chains and justifications

prompt: You are auditing Orion Health Systems' SOC 2 Type II compliance for control CC6.3 (Data Access Authorization). Orion is a healthcare technology company handling PHI. Review all evidence to assess whether data access is authorized based on roles, least privilege, and segregation of duties. Cross-reference the database permissions against the policy, exception register, and access request log. Some apparent issues may have valid exceptions — evaluate each carefully.

findings.title: bi-reporting role has broad read access across 12 production tables but is covered by valid exception EXC-2025-070
findings.description: The database permissions show the bi-reporting role has SELECT access to 12 production tables including patient_records, billing, and prescriptions. This initially appears to violate least-privilege (Section 3.1). However, exception EXC-2025-070 in the exception register documents CISO approval specifically for the BI reporting team's broad read access, with compensating controls including PHI field-level masking via PostgreSQL row-level security policies, query audit logging with 18-month retention, read-only access through a dedicated read replica, and automated alerts for queries returning more than 5,000 rows. The exception was renewed on October 15, 2025, with compensating controls verified.
findings.expected_reasoning: bi-reporting role with broad read access → initially looks like least-privilege violation → check exception register → EXC-2025-070 is active and approved → PHI masking and read replica isolation provide adequate compensating controls → not a finding.

findings.title: User t.brooks (clinical-ops-write role) has INSERT and UPDATE on patient_records with no access request ticket
findings.description: The database permissions show user t.brooks (Tyler Brooks, Clinical Operations) assigned the clinical-ops-write role, which grants INSERT and UPDATE on patient_records, appointments, and treatment_plans. The access request log for Q4 2025 contains no entry for t.brooks — there is no DAR ticket, no approval chain, and no justification documented. Policy Section 3.2 requires all data access to be authorized through a formal request with Data Owner approval for PHI tables. Additionally, the clinical-ops-write role does not appear in the approved RBAC matrix (Section 3.1) — it appears to be a custom role created outside the governed process. The exception register has no entry for this user or role.
findings.expected_reasoning: t.brooks has write access to patient_records → no access request ticket exists → clinical-ops-write is not in the approved RBAC matrix → no exception → ungoverned write access to PHI → critical for healthcare company.

findings.title: Access request DAR-Q4-2025-017 approved by Manager only — missing Data Owner and Privacy Officer approval for PHI access
findings.description: Access request DAR-Q4-2025-017 granted user k.shah (Karen Shah, Data Science) SELECT access to patient_records and prescriptions. The access request log shows this was approved by Manager only (Dr. Michael Torres). Policy Section 3.2 requires Data Owner + Privacy Officer + CISO approval for any access to PHI tables. The approval chain was incomplete — Manager approval alone is insufficient for Restricted/PHI data access. The database permissions confirm k.shah has the access, meaning it was provisioned despite the incomplete approval chain.
findings.expected_reasoning: DAR-Q4-2025-017 for k.shah → patient_records and prescriptions are PHI → policy requires Data Owner + Privacy Officer + CISO approval → only Manager approved → provisioned anyway → broken approval workflow.

findings.title: Exception EXC-2025-065 for svc-legacy-sync expired October 31 but write access remains active
findings.description: The exception register shows EXC-2025-065 (temporary write access for legacy system data synchronization) expired on October 31, 2025 with status 'Expired.' The database permissions show svc-legacy-sync still has INSERT and UPDATE on patient_records and billing as of December 20, 2025 — nearly 2 months after the exception expired. The exception's review_outcome states 'Expired — migration to FHIR API completed. Access should be reverted to read-only.' The access was never reverted.
findings.expected_reasoning: EXC-2025-065 expired Oct 31 → database shows svc-legacy-sync still has write access Dec 20 → exception explicitly states access should be reverted → write access persists 50 days past expiration → failed exception lifecycle management.
